overall_summary: The project is finalizing a major transition to Eliza Cloud Apps with focused infrastructure hardening and security audits. Community activity centers on new plugin submissions and high-level architectural collaboration to support autonomous AI agents.
key_facts: PR #8294 was submitted by naked_monk to add the @usenami/plugin-signer, a keyless CEX/DEX signer using AWS Nitro Enclave, to the third-party registry.
The Eliza Cloud infrastructure transition is currently blocked by a Google OAuth redirect allowlist issue tracked in issue #8257.
Developer doniyorv7503 reported completing a technical audit of the @elizaos/plugin-discord architecture, specifically voice-handling under load.
PR #8277 refactored the 'plugin-lifeops' monolith into six specialized modular plugins including plugin-relationships and plugin-goals.
A multi-project Hetzner deployment pattern was implemented to bypass server quota limits during cloud scaling.
CallTyro was announced as a new free agent registry for automated discovery of ElizaOS agents.
The AI16Z token migration window has officially closed as confirmed by community moderator odilitime on June 7.
Implementation of automated teardown and slot-release for per-tenant databases has been completed to prevent resource leaks.
open_questions: What additional changes are needed from the contributor side after the initial submission of PR #8294?
How should the project measure agent quality for open-ended tasks as requested by builderradmin?

new_issues_prs.title: Eliza Cloud Apps: scaling + ingress gaps before go-live
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: Identifies critical P0 blockers for the Product 2 go-live including ingress and connection limits.

new_issues_prs.title: refactor(lifeops): split plugin-lifeops into focused plugins
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Major architectural cleanup decomposing a 7.5K-LOC monolith into modular components.

new_issues_prs.title: feat(plugin-task-coordinator): odysseus-style orchestrator UI
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: Adds a complete chat-shell UI for multi-agent orchestration.

overall_focus.claim: The project is currently focused on hardening the Eliza Cloud data plane, ensuring per-tenant isolation, and resolving infrastructure bottlenecks in the Hetzner/Terraform workflows.

discord_updates.summary: Clarification of the third-party plugin review process. Submissions now target the main repository with an estimated 48-hour review turnaround by Shaw's agent.
Introduction of high-level quantitative and TradFi architects offering orchestration optimization and state management services.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users expressed a need for better benchmarking of trading agents, specifically distinguishing between complexity and actual performance during market downturns.

strategic_insights.insight: The refactoring of 'lifeops' into domain-specific plugins indicates a strategic shift away from monolithic architecture to facilitate community-driven feature scaling.
implications_or_questions: Does the current registry handle the discovery of these new granular plugins effectively?

strategic_insights.insight: The reliance on AWS Nitro Enclaves for the USENAMI plugin and the push for PKCE/Steward OAuth reflect tightening enterprise security requirements for Eliza Cloud.
implications_or_questions: Will this increase the barrier to entry for smaller developers?

market_analysis.observation: Introduction of Cabal-Hunter, a pay-per-query MCP server for on-chain funding tracing, targeting crypto rug detection.
market_analysis.relevance: Provides a commercial value-add for trading agents built on ElizaOS and addresses specific crypto security market needs.
